pattern: The ordering property of a search tree lets you discard half the tree at each step: compare the target with the current value and descend left or right instead of scanning every node.
hints: You do not have to inspect every node; the tree's left/right arrangement tells you which side a value must be on.
If the target is smaller than the current node's value move to its left child, if larger move right, and stop when they match.
Walk down from the root following that comparison; return the node you land on when its value equals the target, or report nothing once you fall off the tree.

edge_inputs.description: target sits at an internal node and returns its whole subtree
target is absent so the result is empty
target is the root itself, returning the entire tree
target is a leaf reached only by going right repeatedly
value below the minimum present, falling off the left spine

stress.description: random BST from a shuffled permutation, target chosen present or absent
stress.note: insertion order keeps the level-order array a valid BST
